name: email-notify
description: Send SMTP email notifications after Codex completes a task. Use when one Codex or Claude run is finished, or when you need to notify on task completion with device name, project name, status, and summary via email.
capabilities: Send SMTP email notifications with task completion status
Include device name, project name, and summary in emails
Read project name from AGENTS.md configuration
Support TLS and SSL email encryption
Dry-run mode for testing without sending emails
author: caopulan
securitySummary: This skill is a straightforward SMTP email notification utility. It reads environment variables for SMTP configuration, reads AGENTS.md for optional project name extraction, and sends task completion notifications via standard Python smtplib. All detected patterns are expected functionality - email sending is the stated purpose, environment access is for configuration, and filesystem reads are for project name resolution. No malicious behavior confirmed.
valueStatement: You need to know when your AI assistant finishes important tasks. This skill sends email notifications with device name, project name, status, and result summary directly to your inbox.
limitations: SMTP server credentials must be configured manually
No real-time interactive responses, only notifications
Cannot receive or process incoming emails

useCases.title: Monitor automated pipelines
useCases.description: Get notified when automated coding tasks complete on remote servers or CI/CD pipelines.

useCases.title: Track long-running builds
useCases.description: Receive email alerts when complex refactoring or code generation tasks finish on your machine.

useCases.title: Oversee multiple projects
useCases.description: Stay informed about task completion across different projects without checking each one manually.

promptTemplates.title: Basic notification
promptTemplates.prompt: Use skill email-notify to send an email when this task is finished. Task title: 'Code refactoring complete', Status: success, Summary: 'Refactored 15 files with improved type hints.'

promptTemplates.title: Failure alert
promptTemplates.prompt: If the build fails, use email-notify to alert the team. Task title: 'Build failed', Status: failed, Summary: 'Compilation errors in 3 modules due to missing dependencies.'

promptTemplates.title: Multi-recipient
promptTemplates.prompt: Notify the entire team on task completion using email-notify. Task title: 'Weekly report generated', Status: success, Summary: 'Generated 25-page report with performance metrics and recommendations.'

promptTemplates.title: Dry-run test
promptTemplates.prompt: Test the email notification setup by running email-notify in dry-run mode. Task title: 'Test notification', Status: success, Summary: 'This is a test message to verify SMTP configuration.'

bestPractices: Configure SMTP settings in shell profile for persistent environment variables
Use separate email accounts for notifications to protect primary credentials
Test with dry-run mode before relying on notifications for important tasks
antiPatterns: Including secrets or sensitive data in the task summary field
Using personal email credentials for automated notifications
Skipping the dry-run test when first configuring the skill

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Add the instruction to your AGENTS.md file to enable it.

faq.question: What SMTP settings are required?
faq.answer: You need host, port, from address, and to addresses. Username and password are optional for unauthenticated servers.

faq.question: Can I send to multiple recipients?
faq.answer: Yes, separate multiple email addresses with commas or semicolons in the CODEX_EMAIL_TO environment variable.

faq.question: Is my data sent to third parties?
faq.answer: No, emails go directly to your configured SMTP server. No data is collected or stored by this skill.

faq.question: Why is my notification not sending?
faq.answer: Check that all required environment variables are set, verify SMTP credentials, and try dry-run mode to debug configuration issues.

faq.question: How is this different from other notification skills?
faq.answer: This skill uses plain SMTP for maximum compatibility with any email provider, requiring no external API keys or cloud services.
